messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "channel-calendar-lockout",
 "title": "Distributor line-cards and integrator qualification run on annual calendars the 12-month plan cannot intersect",
 "narrative": "35% of the modeled funnel flows through OEM/integrator (0.22) and distributor (0.13) cells — both admitted internal estimates with zero evidence attached. The reference class says both channels have a calendar: distributor line-card reviews are annual events requiring certifications, stock commitments and demand proof in hand; integrators demonstrably select branded incumbents on certification, DCIM compatibility and support (every case study found — Vertiv/Greenergy, Legrand/MIND Park — shows brand selection, none shows a new entrant winning a slot). A pre-launch vendor with no certs on day 0 misses the current listing cycle entirely and enters the next one as an unproven applicant. Within the YAM window these two cells are not small — they are closed, and the model treats 'closed this year' as indistinguishable from 'open at 35%'.",
 "category": "execution",
 "targetNodes": [
  "cust.oem",
  "cust.distributor"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "Distributor line-card onboarding is an annual-cycle gate requiring completed certifications and reference installs at application time — assets a pre-launch entrant does not possess in month 1 and cannot assemble before the cycle closes",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:cust.distributor",
    "profile:Stage",
    "digest:serviceable-benchmark#c13"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "The parallel OEM/integrator path is gated the same way but on brand: integrators select PDU suppliers on certification, DCIM compatibility and support track record, a pattern consistent across the corpus's integrator case studies and the incumbent vendor sets that dominate every published landscape",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:cust.oem",
    "digest:cr3-vendors#c4",
    "digest:cr3-vendors#c7"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "cust.oem (0.22) and cust.distributor (0.13) together carry 35% of the funnel into SAM, but within the first 12 months both cells are calendar-foreclosed; the SAM the Year-1 obtainable percentage is applied to is materially smaller than modeled",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:cust.oem",
    "ledger:cust.distributor"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "Channel shares look like a mix question — 'we'll get some of each' — so the risk review debates the percentages, not their availability. The hidden feature is that indirect channels are not continuously open markets: they are gated queues with annual admission, and a pre-launch company applies to the queue rather than selling into the cell. Nothing in a static share number signals that the cell has an opening date.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "A signed CE master-distributor listing or OEM/integrator supply agreement with committed volumes, effective within the venture's first six months.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.4,
  "rationale": "Annual line-card cycles and brand-based integrator selection are standard for the reference class (industrial electrical hardware entering EU distribution); uncertainty is whether an aggressive pre-launch certification push can compress one cycle, which some Asian PDU entrants have managed with EU-stock commitments.",
  "basis": "base-rate"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "cust.distributor",
   "op": "exclude",
   "value": null,
   "note": "Distributor cell unavailable within the YAM window — listing cycle cannot complete inside 12 months from a zero-cert start"
  },
  {
   "nodeId": "cust.oem",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.11,
   "note": "Integrator cell half-foreclosed: brand/certification selection excludes the entrant from established integrator programs; residual is opportunistic white-label volume"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Line-card additions in rack-power categories at CE electrical/IT distributors",
   "where": "Rexel, Sonepar, Also/Ingram product-catalog update announcements and new-vendor press releases",
   "threshold": "Zero new rack-PDU vendors added in the trailing two quarters signals a hard-gated cycle",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Integrator RFI/qualification requirements for PDU suppliers",
   "where": "Vendor-qualification questionnaires from CE rack integrators and modular-DC builders (e.g., responses to the venture's own outreach, logged monthly)",
   "threshold": "Certification-complete and ≥2 reference installs required at application in >75% of responses",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Interview three CE distributors/integrators now (the ledger's own pending evidence) to map listing-cycle dates, entry requirements, and whether a 'new vendor' fast-track exists",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "cust.oem",
   "note": "Simultaneously prices the unsourced 0.22/0.13 cells and establishes whether the calendar can be intersected at all in year 1"
  },
  {
   "action": "Concentrate Year-1 entirely on the direct mid-operator/enterprise cells and treat channel listings as a Year-2 milestone with pre-work (cert dossier, EU stock plan) started in month 1",
   "type": "operational",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Stops the plan from booking revenue from cells that have no opening date inside the window"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "base-rate-analogy",
 "riskId": "risk.base-rate-analogy.channel-calendar-lockout"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

A pre-launch vendor with no certs on day 0 misses the current listing cycle entirely and enters the next one as an unproven applicant. residual is opportunistic white-label volume"}],"indicators":[{"signal":"Line-card additions in rack-power categories at CE electrical/IT distributors","where":"Rexel, Sonepar, Also/Ingram product-catalog update announcements and new-vendor press releases","threshold":"Zero new rack-PDU vendors added in the trailing two quarters signals a hard-gated cycle","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Integrator RFI/qualification requirements for PDU suppliers","where":"Vendor-qualification questionnaires from CE rack integrators and modular-DC builders (e.g., responses to the venture's own outreach, logged monthly)","threshold":"Certification-complete and ≥2 reference installs required at application in >75% of responses","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Interview three CE distributors/integrators now (the ledger's own pending evidence) to map listing-cycle dates, entry requirements, and whether a 'new vendor' fast-track exists","type":"information","voiNodeId":"cust.oem","note":"Simultaneously prices the unsourced 0.22/0.13 cells and establishes whether the calendar can be intersected at all in year 1"},{"action":"Concentrate Year-1 entirely on the direct mid-operator/enterprise cells and treat channel listings as a Year-2 milestone with pre-work (cert dossier, EU stock plan) started in month 1","type":"operational","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Stops the plan from booking revenue from cells that have no opening date inside the window"}]}
